title: Add order summary and duplicate detection
author: eval-bot

context_units.diff_patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+from models import Order, User
+
+
+def order_summaries(session, user_ids):
+    summaries = []
+    for user_id in user_ids:
+        user = session.query(User).get(user_id)
+        orders = session.query(Order).filter(Order.user_id == user_id).all()
+        summaries.append({"user": user.name, "order_count": len(orders)})
+    return summaries
+
+
+def find_duplicates(items):
+    dupes = []
+    for i in range(len(items)):
+        for j in range(len(items)):
+            if i != j and items[i] == items[j] and items[i] not in dupes:
+                dupes.append(items[i])
+    return dupes

raw_diff_chunks.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+from models import Order, User
+
+
+def order_summaries(session, user_ids):
+    summaries = []
+    for user_id in user_ids:
+        user = session.query(User).get(user_id)
+        orders = session.query(Order).filter(Order.user_id == user_id).all()
+        summaries.append({"user": user.name, "order_count": len(orders)})
+    return summaries
+
+
+def find_duplicates(items):
+    dupes = []
+    for i in range(len(items)):
+        for j in range(len(items)):
+            if i != j and items[i] == items[j] and items[i] not in dupes:
+                dupes.append(items[i])
+    return dupes

expected_findings.title: N+1 query pattern in order_summaries
expected_findings.rationale: Each loop iteration issues two queries per user; this should be a single joined/bulk query to avoid N+1 database round-trips.

expected_findings.title: O(n^2) duplicate detection
expected_findings.rationale: Nested loops plus a membership check make this O(n^2)/O(n^3); a set-based pass is O(n).
